retry_reason: The put operation is unconditional on its execution path and should never fail; retrying would indicate a double-put bug.
contract_summary: This function serves as the seq_file stop callback for a BPF TCP iterator. It releases any remaining socket references stored in the iterator batch (by calling bpf_iter_tcp_put_batch) and optionally runs the BPF program with a stop meta when v is NULL. The put ensures that all sockets acquired during iteration are eventually dereferenced, preventing leaks. The caller (seq_file infrastructure) has no additional obligations after this call; the iterator state is fully cleaned up.

check.reason: The put operation is performed via bpf_iter_tcp_put_batch(iter), where iter = seq->private. The access path seq.private.batch[cur_sk].sk.sk_refcnt.refs correctly extends from the function parameter seq to the refcount field, matching the callee's path iter.batch[cur_sk].sk.sk_refcnt.refs. The type chain seq_file--bpf_tcp_iter_state--bpf_tcp_iter_batch_item--sock--refcount_t--atomic_t has the same number of segments (6) as the path, and the member types align with the struct definitions.
